steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The underlined sentence begins with “But,” signaling a direct contrast to the first sentence's presentation of Nixon as primarily defined by the Watergate scandal.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The underlined sentence presents evidence of Nixon's complex legacy and achievements to challenge the incomplete perception established in the opening.

The other choices misidentify the function:
  • A compares two figures (only discussed here is Nixon)
  • C focuses on associates (unaddressed in the underlined sentence)
  • D explains why accomplishments are overlooked (whereas this sentence highlights them)
.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
